mnemonics.title: OS Services Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: PICK-FACE: Program execution, I/O, Communication, Kernel/Protection, File system, Allocation/Error, Control/Communication, Equipment/Device management

mnemonics.title: System Call Types
mnemonics.content: PEDI-FIC: Process control, Error detection, Device management, Information maintenance, File management, Inter-process communication, Communication/Protection

mnemonics.title: fork() Return Values
mnemonics.content: PARENT gets CHILD PID, CHILD gets ZERO, ERROR gets -1

acronyms.usage: Set of function definitions that programs can use to access OS services
IEEE standard for OS compatibility providing standard system calls
System software managing hardware and providing services to applications
Mechanism for processes to communicate via system calls like pipes, messages, shared memory

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a wall between user space (your app) and kernel space (OS core). A system call is like ringing a doorbell - you make a request (trap/interrupt), the OS kernel answers, performs the service, and returns results back to your app.
Think of it like a building: User mode is the lobby where regular visitors can go, while Kernel mode is the secure server room - only authorized (OS) personnel can enter and control critical systems.
Visualize fork() as making a photocopy - you (parent) make a copy of yourself (child). Both have the same document (code) but are separate individuals with different identities (PIDs).

analogies.analogy: System calls are like a restaurant kitchen - you (user program) place an order (system call), the waiter passes it to the kitchen (OS kernel), the chef prepares it (performs service), and your meal is returned (results).
A file descriptor is like a library card number - you don't need to carry the whole book, just the reference number that the library (OS) uses to find your file.
Kernel mode is like root access in an organization - only the CEO (OS kernel) can access everything and make critical decisions, while employees (user programs) have limited access.
API is like a restaurant menu (lists what's available), while system call is actually ordering and receiving the food - the menu describes what's possible, the order makes it happen.
